Margit

entry.title: How to perform mass upload from an Excel file in a FIORI App in SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Part 2
entry.content:
Introduction

In continuation to my previous blog post (How to perform mass upload from an Excel file in a FIORI App in SAP BTP ABAP Environment - Part 1), in this blog post, I will showcase an easy way to extend a FIORI Elements-based OData V4 application in Business Application Studio (BAS) to add a custom action on the responsive table header of the list report page. This custom action will allow the user to open a dialog to browse local files and upload an Excel file. 

In this blog post, I will be creating a Fiori Elements-based application using the tools provided in SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) and will be extending generated apps using App Extensions to trigger OData V4 operations (in this example, RAP Static Action 'fileUpload' to upload an Excel file and RAP Static Function 'downloadFile' to download an Excel template).

Prerequisites

  • SAP Business Application Studio (BAS) subscription and developer role in your respective BTP Subaccount.
  • Basic knowledge of performing App Extensions to extend generated FIORI Elements based apps.
  • Basic UI5 skill.
  • Use of File Uploader control.

Basic of OData V4 Operation

Function - Functions are operations exposed by an OData service that don't have side effects. Functions must return data and can include additional path segments. Functions are invoked using HTTP method GET.

Action - Actions are operation exposed by an OData service that can have side effects. Actions can return data but must not be composed with additional path segments. Actions are invoked using HTTP method POST.

Operation - Both Functions and Actions are operations that can return data. Operations are either bound to a resource (for example, an entity type), that makes them members of that instance type. Operations can also be unbound. Unbound operations are called as static operations (using “action imports” or “function imports”) since a static (unbound) operation can't be called directly.

The OData V4 model supports OData operations (Action Import, Function Import, Bound Actions and Bound Functions).

RAP - Non-Standard Operations

Non-standard operations are RAP BO Operations that provide transactional behavior and that are user defined and implemented. There are two kinds of non-standard operations.

  1. Action: User-implemented operations that change the data of a BO instance. They are self-implemented operations. Two main categories of actions can be implemented in RAP:

    1. Non-factory actions: Defines a RAP action which offers non-standard behavior. The custom logic must be implemented in the RAP handler method FOR MODIFY. An action per default relates to a RAP BO entity instance and changes the state of the instance. An action is related to an instance by default. If the optional keyword static is used, the action is defined as static action. Static actions are not bound to any instance of a RAP BO entity but relate to the complete entity.

    2. Factory actions: Factory actions are used to create RAP BO entity instances. Factory actions can be instance-bound (default) or static. Instance-bound factory actions can copy specific values of an instance. Static factory actions can be used to create instances with prefilled default values.

  2. Function: User-implemented operations that return data without any side effects.
    1. Instance Function: Bound to an instance of a RAP BO Entity which can have input parameters.
    2. Static Function: Not bound to any instance of a RAP BO Entity but relate to the complete entity. It can also have input parameters.

Can we create unbound OData V4 operations "Action Import" or "Function Import" in RAP?

At this moment, we cannot implement unbound function and action in RAP. Both the static action and functions are related to the complete entity. But it is supported in CAP.

Code sample:

How RAP Static Action and Functions are related to the complete entity?

Let's understand from the metadata itself.

It is bound to the complete entity and with the collection of the entity. 

Let's begin

For the interest of time and topic, I am not showing all the basic steps to create a FIORI Elements based application in BAS. There are multiple blog posts and SAP Learning materials available to refer. I will directly jump to perform app extension.

We will be using the Fiori Guided Development tool to add a custom action to a page using extension and will be using the page map to perform List Report page controller extension.

Note: When you use the Fiori Guided Development tool to add a custom action in OData V4 based app, the framework automatically adds a custom action handler function in JavaScript creating a new custom JS file but does not perform controller extension. This is the way OData V4 app extension behaves which adds a "controlConfiguration" in the target page.

Custom Action in the Table toolbar for the list report in OData V2 vs OData V4:

OData V2:

OData V4:

Further Reference: Adding Custom Actions Using Extension Points 
But why do we need a controller extension? Can't we use the custom action handler function created automatically by the Guided Development tool in an OData V4 app?

We need to create a controller extension in an OData V4 app explicitly to use the SAP FIORI Elements extension API to open a dialog using the 'loadFragement' function; to perform OData operations and to use Edit Flow (A controller extension offering hooks into the edit flow of the application) to invoke action and perform secured execution. Otherwise, within the custom JS file, we won't get the model reference to trigger the OData operation (here RAP Static Action and Static Function).

So, we will start by adding a custom action to the table toolbar using the FIORI Guided Development tool.

Step 1: Right click on the 'webapp' folder and select 'Open Guided Development'

Step 2: Select 'Add a custom action to a page using extensions'.

Step 3: Follow the guide and select the page and choose a custom controller and function name.

Following code snippets to be inserted.

Go ahead and insert snippets. This will modify the manifest settings and create custom JS file.

Step 4: But we need to perform controller extension. So, the custom JS file can be deleted. 

Step 5: Now, we will perform the List report controller extension using the page map. So, right click on 'webapp' folder and select 'Show Page Map'.

Step 6: From the page map, select the List Report page and click on 'Show controller extension'. After that, click on 'Add Controller Extension' to create controller extension on the list report page.

Note: It is advisable to perform controller extensions for the concerned page rather than performing a generic extension for all the list report pages. Here, 'VendorEmailList' is the target list report page. So, I selected that option.

Step 7: Now, we can navigate to the controller extension using 'Edit in source code' button and add custom logic.

This the way the controller extension looks.

Let us add a custom handler function 'uploadMailDialog' to handle the custom action.

After the controller extension, the following changes applied to the manifest.json file. It signifies a controller extension added to the list report controller.

Step 8: Now, we need to tweak the manifest settings in such a way that the custom action triggers the custom handler function 'uploadMailDialog' added to the controller extension.

We need to add the custom action path adding '.extension' as a prefix. '.extension' refers to the controller extension.

Step 9: Now, if we preview the application and click on the custom action button, it will trigger the controller extension.

Step 10: Let us now add a fragment to open the file dialog. So, we will use 'fileUploader' control. I created a fragment folder inside the 'ext' folder and added a new xml file 'uploadFileDialog.fragement.xml'.

Following is the sample code snippet.

 

 

<core:FragmentDefinition xmlns:core="sap.ui.core" xmlns:f="sap.ui.layout.form" xmlns:u="sap.ui.unified" xmlns="sap.m"
	xmlns:layout="sap.ui.layout">
	<Dialog id="idFileDialog" title="{i18n>uploadVendorMailDialogTitle}" >
		
		<VBox id="idVBox" width="100%">
			<core:InvisibleText id="idInvisibleText" text="{i18n>uploadVendorMailDialogTitle}"/>
			<f:SimpleForm id="idSimpleForm" editable="true" layout="ResponsiveGridLayout" maxContainerCols="2">
				<f:content>
					<Label id="idFileUploadlabel" required="true" text="{i18n>uploadVendorMailFile}"/>
					<u:FileUploader id="idFileUpload" name="internalMailFileUpload" change="onFileChange" width="100%" 
                    uploadComplete="onUploadComplete" style="Emphasized" fileType="xls,xlsx" placeholder="{i18n>uploadVendorMailPlaceholder}"
                    tooltip="{i18n>uploadVendorMailTooolTip}" sendXHR="false" />
				</f:content>
			</f:SimpleForm>
		</VBox>
		<footer>
			<Toolbar id="idFooterToolbar">
				<content>
					<Button id="idDownloadTempButton" text="{i18n>downloadTempButtonTxt}" press="onTempDownload" icon= "sap-icon://download-from-cloud" />
					<Button id="idUploadButton" text="{i18n>uploadButtonTxt}" type="Emphasized" press="onUploadPress" icon="sap-icon://upload-to-cloud" />
					<Button id="idCancelButton" text="{i18n>cancelButtonTxt}" press="onCancelPress" icon="sap-icon://cancel"/>
				</content>
			</Toolbar>
		</footer>
	</Dialog>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

 

Now, add the logic to open the dialog on click of the action similar to the code snippet mentioned below.

 

 

		uploadMailDialog: function (oEvent) {
			this.base.getExtensionAPI().loadFragment({
				name: Constants.fragmentName,
				type: "XML",
				controller: this
			}).then(function (oDialogResult) {
				oDialog = oDialogResult;
				oDialogResult.open();
			})
		},

 

 

Now, in application preview the app looks like below.

Step 11: So, till now, on click of the action 'Upload', a dialog will open up asking the user to upload a file. The button 'Template' will allow users to download an Excel template, and the button 'Upload' will allow users to upload the given file.

Let us add logic to read the file content, mimetype and filename of the given file. I used 'onFileChange' event of the File uploader control as highlighted below.

 

 

		// On File Change
		onFileChange: function (oEvent) {
			// Read file
			var file = oEvent.getParameter("files")[0];
			if (file === undefined) {
				return;
			}
			fileType = file.type;  //mimetype or file type
			fileName = file.name;
			//Instantiate JavaScript FileReader API
			var fileReader = new FileReader();
			//Read file content using JavaScript FileReader API
			var readFile = function onReadFile(file) {
				return new Promise(function (resolve) {
					fileReader.onload = function (loadEvent) {
						resolve(loadEvent.target.result.match(/,(.*)$/)[1]);
					};
					fileReader.readAsDataURL(file);
				});
			};

			new Action(readFile(file)).executeWithBusyIndicator().then(function (result) {
				fileContent = result;
			})
		},

 

 

Here, I used JS FileReader API to read the file content as data URL and later used RegEx to clean the base64 encoded file content. 

Step 12: Now, implement the logic to upload a file once the 'upload' button is clicked.

 

 

		//perform upload
		onUploadPress: function (oEvent) {
			var oResourceBundle = this.base.getView().getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle();
			//check file has been entered
			if (fileContent === undefined || fileContent === "") {
				MessageToast.show(oResourceBundle.getText("uploadFileErrMsg"));
				return;
			}

			var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel();

			var oOperation = oModel.bindContext("/VendorEmail" + Constants.serviceNamespace + "fileUpload(...)");

			var fnSuccess = function () {
				oModel.refresh();
				MessageToast.show(oResourceBundle.getText("uploadFileSuccMsg"));
				oDialog.close();
				//Clear the file name from file uploader
				sap.ui.getCore().byId("idFileUpload").clear();
				oDialog.destroy();
				fileContent = undefined;
			}.bind(this);

			var fnError = function (oError) {
				this.base.editFlow.securedExecution(
					function () {
						Messaging.addMessages(
							new sap.ui.core.message.Message({
								message: oError.message,
								target: "",
								persistent: true,
								type: sap.ui.core.MessageType.Error,
								code: oError.error.code
							})
						);
						var aErrorDetail = oError.error.details;
						aErrorDetail.forEach((error) => {
							Messaging.addMessages(
								new sap.ui.core.message.Message({
									message: error.message,
									target: "",
									persistent: true,
									type: sap.ui.core.MessageType.Error,
									code: error.code
								})
							);
						})
					}
				);
				oDialog.close();
				//Clear the file name from file uploader
				sap.ui.getCore().byId("idFileUpload").clear();
				oDialog.destroy();
				fileContent = undefined;
			}.bind(this);

			oOperation.setParameter("mimeType", fileType);
			oOperation.setParameter("fileName", fileName);
			oOperation.setParameter("fileContent", fileContent);
			oOperation.setParameter("process", sProcess);
			oOperation.execute().then(fnSuccess, fnError);
		},

 

 

Here, I triggered OData V4 operation a bound action (RAP 'fileUpload' Static Action) passing File Type, File Name and File Content as parameters.

Earlier, I already explained that a RAP Static Action or Function is bound to a collection specified by an OData entity set. So, to call such actions or functions, you can create a context binding with an absolute path, or with a relative path for the operation (for example odata.srv.name.space.staticMyAction(...)") and the header context of a list binding as parent context. The following sample shows a button press event handler which calls the static MyAction action on the MyEntity entity set.

 

var oModel = this.getView().getModel();
 
oModel.bindContext("/MyEntity/odata.srv.name.space.staticMyAction(...)").invoke();

 

The same example with a relative binding and the header context of the list binding as parent context:

 

var oModel = this.getView().getModel(),
    // assume there is a table with ID "idMyEntityTab" and its items aggregation bound to "/MyEntity"
    oListBinding = this.byId("idMyEntityTab").getBinding("items"),
    oHeaderContext = oListBinding.getHeaderContext();

oModel.bindContext("name.space.staticMyAction(...)", oHeaderContext).invoke();

 

Note: In my code sample, I am using "execute" function of the OData V4 Context binding. But, from UI5 version 1.123.0 onwards, "execute" function is deprecated. So, use "invoke" function instead as shown in the example. Simply, replace "execute' function with "invoke".

 

			oOperation.setParameter("mimeType", fileType);
			oOperation.setParameter("fileName", fileName);
			oOperation.setParameter("fileContent", fileContent);
			oOperation.setParameter("process", sProcess);
			oOperation.invoke().then(fnSuccess, fnError);

 

Reference: Execute function of OData V4 Context Binding API 

Alternatively, you can also use the "invokeAction" method of the Edit Flow API ( Edit Flow API ). However, I found the above approach to be the preferred choice.

Reference: How to call RAP static action from elements FPM extension with EditFlow.invokeAction()? 

After this step when you will execute the 'upload' action selecting an Excel file to upload, the RAP static action 'fileUpload' will trigger and the XCO XLSX module will do the rest to parse the file content and create/update BO entity instances as explained in my previous blog post.

Step 13: Similar to the file upload, the excel template can also be downloaded invoking RAP static function 'downloadFile'.

Sample code snippet:

 

onTempDownload: function (oEvent) {
			var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel(),
				oResourceBundle = this.base.getView().getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle();

			var oModel = this.base.getExtensionAPI().getModel(),
				oResourceBundle = this.base.getView().getModel("i18n").getResourceBundle();

			var oOperation = oModel.bindContext("/VendorEmail" + Constants.serviceNamespace + "downloadFile(...)");

			//Success function to display success messages from OData Operation
			var fnSuccess = function () {
				var oResults = oOperation.getBoundContext().getObject();

				var aUint8Array = Uint8Array.from(atob(oResults.fileContent), c => c.charCodeAt(0)),
					oblob = new Blob([aUint8Array], { type: oResults.mimeType });

				File.save(oblob, oResults.fileName, oResults.fileExtension, oResults.mimeType);
				MessageToast.show(oResourceBundle.getText("downloadTempSuccMsg"));
			}.bind(this);

			//Error function to display error messages from OData Operation
			var fnError = function () {
				this.base.editFlow.securedExecution(
					function () {
						Messaging.addMessages(
							new sap.ui.core.message.Message({
								message: oError.message,
								target: "",
								persistent: true,
								type: sap.ui.core.MessageType.Error,
								code: oError.error.code
							})
						);
						var aErrorDetail = oError.error.details;
						aErrorDetail.forEach((error) => {
							Messaging.addMessages(
								new sap.ui.core.message.Message({
									message: error.message,
									target: "",
									persistent: true,
									type: sap.ui.core.MessageType.Error,
									code: error.code
								})
							);
						})
					}
				);
			}.bind(this);

			// Execute OData V4 operation i.e a static function 'downloadFile' to download the excel template
			oOperation.execute().then(fnSuccess, fnError)
                        // From UI5 version 1.123.0 onwards use invoke function
			//oOperation.invoke().then(fnSuccess, fnError);

 

Please stay tuned for the next blog post where I will explain how I used the XCO XLSX module to create an Excel template and dynamically handled the column names based on the template structure defined in the backend and populated the text maintained in the data elements. This will enable them to read the translated texts as well. The translation process is different in ABAP Cloud; hence, the traditional approach does not work.

entry.title: Basics and configuration in Service with Advanced Execution Concept in SAP S/4HANA Service
entry.content:
Service with Advanced Execution

Service with Advanced Execution process is used for services which require extensive planning and execution. The core characteristics of this process is the integration of the Service, which is responsible for the commercial part of the process, with the Maintenance Management, which is used for planning and execution of the service. In this process Service is also integrated with Financial Accounting which is responsible to ensure an accurate recording of cost and revenue that occurs in the service transaction.

The goal of this blog post to introduce the basics concept and the configuration of integration part which are required for the Service with Advanced Execution scenario.

Basic concepts in Service with Advanced Execution

Basic concepts

In the Service with Advanced Execution process both Service and Maintenance Management have specific roles.

Service is responsible for the commercial part of the process:

  • Service contract management and price agreement
  • Service order quotation and service order management
  • Pricing and credit check
  • Billing

Maintenance Management is responsible for planning and execution of service:

  • Maintenance task list
  • Maintenance order and confirmation management
  • Maintenance planning with strategy
  • Resource scheduling
  • Advance Shipment

The Service with Advanced Execution can be used in the following scenarios:

  • Preventive Service
    in this scenario Service is responsible to set up a regular service based on the service agreements and Maintenance Management is responsible for the planning and execution based on a maintenance order.
  • Corrective Service
    in this scenario the price (estimated- or final cost) of service is calculated based on the service requirement and sent to the customer in a service quotation for approval. If the service quotation is accepted by the customer a service order is created. The planning and execution are done in a maintenance order.
  • Billable Maintenance
    in this scenario the maintenance needs to be billed to the customer. The maintenance order is created to plan and execute the service and to allow the possibility of billing. The service order is used to generate the billing document after the service is completed.

The item which is created in Service with Advanced Execution process is called Execution Order Item. The Execution Order Item has the following features:

  • Billing relevance
    Depending on the billing scenario the following billing relevance can be set up for an execution order item:
    • Not relevant for billing
    • Summarized Billing
    • Itemized Billing
    • Billing on Completion
    • Billing via Billing Plan After Release
  • Dynamic Items
    DIP profile is entered in the Execution Order Item in the Service Order and used to determine the items and lines in the Debit Memo Request during billing. (The DIP profile is mandatory when the billing relevance is Summarized or Itemized Billing.
  • Planning Plant and Service Organization
    Planning plant and work center are available as additional fields in the execution order item of the service order quotation and service order. These fields are mandatory in the maintenance order and are copied over from the execution order item.
  • Maintenance Order Assignment Block
    The billable Maintenance Order is linked with the Execution Order Item and the Maintenance Order is displayed in the Transaction History in the item details.
  • Advanced Variant Configuration
    Configurable products can be used as Execution Order Items in Service Order Management and in General Maintenance Task List in billable Maintenance Order.
  • Credit Check
    Credit check feature can be used for Execution Order Items  in fixed price billing scenario for the billing relevance Billing on Completion.
  • Intercompany Service Orders
    Execution Order Items can be created as part of Intercompany Service Orders for business transaction between two companies with different company codes that belong to the same organization.

Basic configuration in Service with Advanced Execution process

Integration between Service and Maintenance Management

In order to activate Service with Advanced Execution integrated with Maintenance Management the mapping between Transaction Types and Item Categories used in Service and Order Types used in Maintenance Management has to be specified in Customizing under Service -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration -> Map Order Types

By this Customizing activity the mapping between Service with Advanced Execution and Maintenance Management can be set up.
Under Service to Maintenance Management the transaction type and item category used in Service can be mapped to Maintenance Order type used in Maintenance Management. This mapping is used to determine the Maintenance Order Type during the creation of a Maintenance Order corresponding to an execution order item.
Under Maintenance Management to Service the Maintenance Order Type can be mapped to the transaction type and item category used in Service. By this customizing setting the correct transaction type and item category can be determined during the creation of Service Order corresponding to the Maintenance Order.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration -> Map Priorities

By this Customizing activity the service priorities can be mapped to the plant maintenance priorities. This mapping is used in integration scenarios such as the creation of Maintenance Orders from Service Orders.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Plant Maintenance Integration -> Define Text Types

With this Customizing activity is possible to define text type which can be used to transfer information from the Service Order to the Maintenance Order.

Integration between Service and Finance

The Customizing activity Enable Item-Based Accounting for Service needs to be enabled to allow to use Service with Advanced Execution process.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Integration -> Enable Item-Based Accounting for Service

With this Customizing activity accounting can be activated for Service based on a dedicated cost object. In Item-Based Accounting the service transaction items are recognized as account assignment objects. The costs and revenues are posted to these objects.

By setting the switch to True an accounting based on service transaction items is enabled as dedicated account assignment objects for service transactions.

Ad Hoc Billing for Service with Advanced Execution

Unlike the rules of the periodic or milestone billing plan, Ad Hoc Billing enables to define values and dates freely. The feature can be used for the Service with Advanced Execution scenario is called Billing of Remaining Net Value. This feature enables the system to generate a billing request line with all remaining net value of the Execution Order Item automatically.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan -> Define Billing Plan Types

By this Customizing activity the billing type Ad Hoc Billing can be defined for Service. By using Ad Hoc Billing different amounts at specified dates can be billed to the customer. The system generates on e billing request line for each billing date.

Service -> Transactions -> Settings for Service Transactions -> Basic Settings -> Billing Plan -> Assign Billing Plan Type to Item Category

In this Customizing activity the billing plan type Service: Ad Hoc Billing can be assigned to the item category MSI1 Maint. Service Item which is used in the Maintenance Order.

 

For more information about Service with Advanced Execution process check out the following blog post:

Service with Advanced Execution - SAP S/4HANA Clou... - SAP Community

entry.title: Joule and SAP Business AI Now available for RISE with SAP Customers
entry.content:
SAP’s vision for Business AI is to bring powerful Generative AI capabilities across all our products and services through SAP BTP to benefit the customers in achieving measurable business outcomes. As you are already aware, SAP introduced Joule in Sep 2023, a copilot that truly understands your business revolutionizing the interactions with SAP systems making every touchpoint count and every task simpler in a secure and compliant way. Joule will be embedded across SAP’s cloud enterprise portfolio, delivering proactive and contextualized insights from SAP solutions and third-party sources. Joule is everywhere, was one of the most discussed topics at SAPPHIRE 2024, and needs no introduction, find out everything you need to know about Joule on this blog.

We are excited to announce Joule is now available for RISE with SAP customers with significant enhancements to RISE with SAP commercial packages focused on accelerating digital transformation and bringing the power of AI to all our customers. Joule, our AI copilot, will now be available for all existing and new RISE with SAP customers, natively integrating into SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition. Since launching RISE with SAP in Jan 2021, our mission to transform customers into a cloud operating model had a strong momentum in RISE adoption. We have helped over 6000+ customers across various industries and geographies to transform to a cloud operating model through RISE with SAP among which 3000+ customers went live.

During this journey of cloud transformation, we have observed that it is essential for every enterprise to evolve with the latest technologies in order to adopt them and remain competitive in the market. To maintain the pace with the need for agility and innovation, while moving towards fast-changing technologies, customers need to adapt to changes that are evolving in AI technologies and innovations. As AI and Generative AI have become the top priorities for every enterprise striving to stay competitive and maintain its market position, there are a few challenges that all enterprises must acknowledge and overcome.

Below are the transformational drivers that are impacting customers to be prepared for the future.

  • Rising Customer Expectations: In the digital age, customers are more empowered than ever, driving the demand for exceptional experiences, personalized services, and seamless interactions across various channels.
  • Technological Advancements: The rapid emergence of technologies like generative AI presents both a challenge and an opportunity for businesses to stay ahead by leveraging innovation for growth.
  • Operational Efficiency: Enhancing operational efficiency is crucial for enterprises aiming to streamline processes, reduce costs, and boost productivity.
  • Competitive Pressure: To maintain a competitive edge and secure market position, companies must continuously evolve and adapt to the ever-changing landscape.

To address these challenges, SAP Cloud ERP is infused with AI capabilities to accelerate business ability and meet business goals.

McKinsey reports that Generative AI could generate over $2.6 to $4.4 trillion incremental value annually to the global market. As AI adoption accelerates, particularly in the area of Generative AI, there is an extreme need to capitalize on this technology. Leaders across industries are prioritizing investments and closely tracking the potential impact of Generative AI, recognizing that failure to stay competitive could pose significant risks. CEOs are making these strategic decisions to avoid losing market share and maintaining their competitive edge.

 

Why only cloud? And limitations of On-Premise:

The latest technological advancements like Generative AI need high computation power for efficient and accelerated performance. It requires a level of infrastructure that can handle the speed at which the innovations are changing and, run, activate, and deploy the AI use cases effectively. Data plays a critical role in implementing AI use cases, with the volume of data growing rapidly each day. To efficiently process and fine-tune this data for optimal and accelerated performance, the latest and most powerful infrastructure that can scale according to evolving requirements is essential.

The on-premise environments become challenging when scaling the systems to meet increased AI demand. The computation power for on-premise systems becomes costly and time-consuming. Significant resources and infrastructure are required to grow and maintain hardware and software systems. The on-premise environment presents significant challenges, making a cloud a more effective option considering scalability, speed, and cost-effectiveness.

Benefits to Customers 

By adopting AI, customers can gain benefits that can impact both their Business and People. In the field of business, AI enhances decision-making faster, with data-driven insights and recommendations, ensuring safer and more informed choices. For individuals, AI transforms work through automation and streamlines user experiences, ultimately driving better business outcomes

Benefits of AI for Business:

Reinvent Customer Service with personalized experiences that address customer needs quickly.

Boost Finance Excellence with AI that helps optimize working capital and closing functions.

Drive Operational Excellence by optimizing manufacturing and asset performance and elevating product quality.

Advance Intelligent Order Fulfillment by streamlining and automating the order fulfillment process.

Benefits of AI for People:

Empower End Users to increase efficiency and eliminate redundant tasks ​

Interact with Business using natural language for informational, navigational, and transactional assistance.​

Uncover Insights that would otherwise be challenging or time-consuming to discover 

 

SAP Business AI in RISE with SAP

Aligning with SAP’s Business AI strategy, SAP launched the integration of Joule starting with Success Factors in Dec 2023. Now, Joule is available for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud for RISE with SAP customers. Joule offers a navigational pattern helping in navigating to relevant SAP Fiori apps and letting the user work directly in the app’s web UI for new and occasional users without prior knowledge of individual SAP Fiori apps.

RISE customers will have the advantage of experiencing generative AI capabilities across SAP cloud ERP. All existing and new RISE customers will have access to Joule to quickly access apps, effectively gain insights on business objects, and speedily resolve user issues. There are a few prerequisites to access Joule on SAP S/4HANA quoted below:

Prerequisites for Joule integration with SAP S/4HANA (requires administrator role):

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01 or later.
  • SAP Business Technology Platform, with the following entitlements
    • Joule Entitlements - Joule(das-application), please refer to license topic
    • SAP Workzone, standard edition
    • SAP Cloud Connectivity
    • Cloud Foundry
  • SAP Cloud Identity Services
  • SAP Cloud Connector with Principal Propagation
    • System Mapping to expose the required HTTP protocol

To know more about the capabilities of Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition and complete the configuration guide follow this link.

Commercial Packages

As part of RISE with SAP offerings, entitlements are provided with different customer profile packages like Base Edition, Premium Edition, and Premium Plus Edition. Joule is accessible in all available packages Base, Premium, and Premium Plus for RISE customers. Joule will be accessible by all existing and new RISE customers fulfilling the prerequisites mentioned above to use capabilities. Joule and Generative AI will be part of the new SKU as entitlements under the Artificial Intelligence domain.

RISE Base Edition: Provides small organizations with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition functionalities with a lean setup

RISE Premium Edition: Enables organization’s business transformation supported by built-in AI capabilities into SAP applications, process automation, low-code business application development, and access to supplier networks.

RISE Premium Plus Edition: Extends premium by unlocking more AI scenarios, including sustainability solutions, and advanced finance capabilities.

AI has significant potential to accelerate business processes and optimize decision-making. Since AI is at the top of every enterprise’s priority list, we are constantly innovating to deliver more AI-powered business processes and bringing the capabilities of Business AI as an entitlement as part of the SKU for RISE with SAP. Generative AI capabilities are available for RISE Premium and RISE Premium Plus packages where respective AI units are available based on the package. RISE with SAP Premium customers are provided with AI units to pilot AI-driven innovations like SAP Cash Application, SAP Business Integrity Screening, and SAP Service Management.

SAP AI Unit is a single SKU. SAP’s AI use cases are commercialized as capabilities within a single SKU regardless of LoBs. All customers can license SAP AI Unit to purchase AI units but to be able to use specific AI capabilities, you need to fulfill the licensing pre-requisites.

AI units are like a virtual currency that allows you to consume a wide set of AI use cases across different LoB solutions within SAP which included Joule overages. AI units are based on annual subscriptions and are sold in a block of 100 AI units. The minimum block to purchase is 1 per year and must be used by the end of the year as all unused AI units will be lost if not consumed by the end of the year.

Commercials & Estimator

RISE customers will have entitlements of 2500 Joule messages per FUE per contract year free of charge to interact with SAP A/4HANA Cloud, private edition. Once the entitled number of messages is consumed and you are willing to continue accessing Joule, you can purchase additional messages with AI units SKU. A message is an electronic communication exchanged via the capability of cloud service. For cloud services like Joule, a user request, together with the corresponding response from Joule is considered as 1 message.

Customers can use the AI Unit Estimator to estimate x number of AI units required to run the selected AI capabilities and can request a quote from the estimator directly. After selecting the AI feature, the customer can add the number of transactions (in a block of 10,000) you would need for the selected AI feature. Cost estimation is automatically shown based on the selection and a quote can be created from the same page for the selected AI feature and the transactions based on the requirement.

Another way where customers can access the AI services is from the SAP for Me dashboard under the Portfolio & Products tab by selecting Business AI and activating the AI feature. The features are listed based on the SAP products they belong to, where customers can select whether to activate the feature or not. The customer has the option to view the consumption for the respective feature or calculate cost estimation for the feature by selecting an estimator for the respective feature.

Early Adopter Care (EAC) Program: Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

SAP offers innovation adopter services to help you jump-start your projects with SAP’s latest products and solutions. SAP Early Adopter Care connects customers with products to help them simplify, innovate, and digitize fast. Through the support of the Early Adopter Care project coach, we enable two-way communication, and transparency, and allow stakeholders to minimize project risk.

Benefits of the EAC program:

Support with early implementation of SAP’s latest innovations.

Direct channel to development for product and solution insights.

To join the Early adopter care program for Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition, check out this link. Registration for the EAC program for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition started on 15th July and is open till 31st Dec 2024.

Conditions for participation:

Participation in the early adopter care program is voluntary, at the sole discretion of SAP, and dependent on the following requisites.

  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition 2023 FPS01
  • Plans to use Joule in production
  • Willingness to become a reference
  • The full set of restrictions can be found here General Constraints for Joule & Constraints for Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

Customer Engagement Initiative: AI in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

The SAP Customer Engagement Initiative enables you, as an SAP customer or partner, to get insights into SAP’s product developments and directly work with the developers to define and shape future product directions.

What is in for you?

  • Opportunity to define and shape the product's future.
  • Gain early insights and actively engage to learn and shape the new innovations that matter.
  • Access and engage directly with the product engineering teams.

How to get engaged with us?

Visit this link and register your company for this opportunity.

 

Joule Integration – Step-by-step guide

To configure Joule and integrate with SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition, check the below blogs for a step-by-step guide.

Joule – Getting Started with Joule and SAP SuccessFactors

Joule for SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide

entry.title: Check compatibility pack usage in SAP S/4HANA with SAP EarlyWatch Alert
entry.content:
How-to:

Many compatibility packs will expire at the end of December 2025. Are you aware if you are using compatibility packs in your SAP S/4HANA on-premise or private cloud edition? A new service feature helps you evaluate the risk of becoming non-compliant and losing SAP Support for some of your business processes. The SAP EarlyWatch Alert (EWA) service has introduced a new check and report chapter for your convenience.

Watch this demo video on the topic:  Demo Video

Simply use this link to check your systems: Landscape Check

It will list your systems under the alert "Compatibility Usage Expiry 31.12.2025" if any issues were found. The information is only visible to S-Users in your company and is protected by the SAP EarlyWatch Alert authorisation in SAP for Me. Click on the system IDs to open the report chapter, which lists the transactions, reports, business functions, and releases which will expire. 

Prerequisites:

- SAP S/4HANA on-premise: Your system must send EWA data to SAP. See SAP Note FAQ- Using SAP EarlyWatch Alert.

- SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition: All production systems have already activated the service out-of-the-box.

- You need a valid S-User in SAP for Me with the following authorisations: 

  • Service Reports and Feedback

  • Manage Alert(s) in SAP EarlyWatch Alert for all S-users

Request the authorisation here: Authorization Request 

If you have any issues with authorisations: Get Support on XX-SER-SAPSMP-USR

Chapter Content:

The new report chapter lists the compatibility packages in use over the last three months. If the functionality was used in the last month, a red rating is triggered (which can be removed with the snooze button; see next section). For each Simplification Item, we provide a specific SAP Note with background information on the business process and an alternative way forward. The check does not cover items expiring in 2030 and includes most, but not all, compatibility packs that expire at the end of 2025. For full coverage, please use the SAP Readiness Check. SAP EarlyWatch Alert helps you track the usage on a weekly basis and measure your success in replacing the functionality.

Feedback to SAP: 

Are you already aware of the usage issues and want to let SAP know you are working on a solution? This can be achieved using the Snooze feature in the app Solution Finder:

1. Open the list of alerts: Alert List

2. Navigate to the Alerts tab.

3. Click the Snooze button (stopwatch icon on the right side).

4. Enter your resolution date into the field "Relevant again on:".

5. Choose the reason "In Implementation" or "Upgrade planned" from the drop-down menu.

Now your SAP contacts can see that you are already aware and working on a resolution.

entry.title: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Extend FIORI App "Manage Customer Master" with Z-field
entry.content:
Introduction

Utilize the FIOR app "Custom Fields" (F1481) from the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (ondemand.com) to easily add a Z-field to your FIORI app. This allows for custom field extension without the need for programming, thanks to the Key User Extensibility functionality. For more information on this topic, check out the blog post "SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA – What is Key User Extensibility and Who are your Key Users."

Key User Extensibility is part of Clean Core approach to keep your system/landscape clean as described at blog post Clean Core demystified: What does it mean and how to achieve it with SAP BTP?

More information about Key User Extensibility, including required roles and initial steps, can be found on the SAP Help website.

In the example outlined in this blog, I will demonstrate the addition of a custom field (Z-field) to the "Manage Customer Master" FIORI app (F0850A) at the Sales Area level. The custom field, "Last Phone Contact," will be added as an example. This information can be found in the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library.

This extension will be done in 2 steps:
1. Z-field definition
2. Z-field assignment at FIORI app

Target audience: Functional expert, Development expert

Starting point

In the FIORI app "Manage Customer Master," a new Z-field should be added to the Sales Area level to store the last phone contact date specific to the Sales Area. This field should be located in the "General Data" section (highlighted in red).

 

Z-field definition

To define a custom Z-field run FIORI app "Custom Fields".

At this app you will see all so far created custom fields. To add a new field push "Create" button.

 

Field properties

At appearing pop-up you have to define your field properties like:
1. Business Context
2. Label
3. Identifier
4. Tooltip
5. Type

Business Context

With setting the Business Context you tell the system to which objects the Z-field is related to.

Following Business Contexts are relevant for Business Partner, Customer or Supplier:

BP_CUSTVEND1                      - Master Data: Business Partner BUT000
CUSTOMER_GENERAL            - Customer Core View KNA1
CUSTOMER_GEN_ADDR         - Customer General Address Dependent Context KNA1_ADDR
CUSTOMER_SALES_ADDR     - Customer Sales Address Dependent Context KNVV_ADDR
CUST_COMPANYCODE           - Customer company code core KNB1
CUST_SALES                           - Partner Sales area core view KNVV
SUPLR_PURORG_ADDR         - Supplier Purchasing Organisation Address Dependent Context  -> Listed but not available
SUPPLIER_GENERAL              - Supplier Core View LFA1
SUPPLIER_GEN_ADDR           - Supplier General Address Dependent Context  -> Listed but not available
SUP_COMPANYCODE             - Supplier Company Code Core LFB1
SUP_PURORG                         - Supplier Purchasing Org Core LFM1

The SCFD_REGISTRY transaction allows you to determine the objects associated with the business context, such as CDS Views, OData Models, and BAdIs, through the Custom Fields: Registry UI.

You can locate CDS View MD_CUSTOMER_MASTER_SRV_01 in the FIORI Library for the FIORI app "Manage Customer Master" (F0850A) by referring to the Implementation Information section of the SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (ondemand.com).

 

Label

This field description is displayed on screens and includes technical details.

 

Identifier

This is the technical name of this field starting with a predefined prefix.

 

Tooltip

This is the description which is shown if you hoover over this field with your mouse.

 

Type

This field is the technical type that can be found in the SE11 transaction. The screenshot shows the data I entered.

I recommend adjusting or at least checking the predefined usages of this new field. To do this, press the "Create and Edit" button.

You can view additional field descriptions such as Medium Label and different tabs like "User Interface" at the detail screen. These tabs display potential technical objects. The number within the tab description indicates how many, for example, User Interfaces are assigned to your selected Business Context.

You may choose to establish or leave unused a new field on each technical object. In order to publish the new field for the FIORI app "Manage Customer Master", you must enable it for the User Interface Manage Customer Master, which is based on the Data Source MD_CUSTOMER_MASTER_SRV_01.

Please note that it is not possible to enable this field for any GUI transaction. If you wish to add this new field to a BP transaction, please refer to the blog post "SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Field Enhancement" on SAP Community for instructions. You can find the relevant information under the "Implementation" topic.

To make this field available for SOAP APIs and/or IDOCs, you should enable it in these areas.

After defining technical usages, you can add field description translations by navigating to the "General Information" tab.

Select target language(s) and enter texts in target language.

All preparations are complete, and you can now save your work. Saving the setup allows you to preserve your work in a temporary state, enabling you to make further refinements and save again. When you click the "Publish" button, the system will initiate the generation process, automatically creating or adjusting all technical objects in the background.

The app will automatically navigate to the view of custom fields overview. You will need to scroll all the way down to the end of the field list to see your Z-field with the status "Publishing…"

Keep in mind that it will take some time to publish the new field. The more data you have in your database, the longer it will take. This is due to the column store storage type of the HANA database, which requires complex restructuring during extensions. Additionally, the decompression and recompression of data for compression prolong the process, and indexes need to be recreated or reorganized for new columns, adding to the time it takes. After the publishing is finalized, the screen will be refreshed and you will see the new field:

Now your new field is ready to use.

Technical field check

Check with field Viewer

Run Transaction SCFD_FIELDVIEWER to check your new field.

Check with SE11

To check the usage of this new field navigate to tab "Usages". Here you can see all objects which has been enhanced by this field.

For example let's have a look at enhanced objects like table KNVV - Customer Sales Area Data
Run transaction SE11 and display table KNVV.

There are important structures which has been enhanced automatically. I will stick to 2 examples:
1. Migration structure for customer master data
2. Main CVI structure CVIS_EI_EXTERN

Run transaction SE11 and display structure MIG_CMDS_EI_CUST_SALES_EXT_S which is part of migration structure MIG_CMDS_EI_CUST_SALES_EXT_T which is used at function module CMD_MIG_BP_CVI_CREATE.
(Navigation path from function module import parameter: IT_EXT_CUST_SALES -> MIG_CMDS_EI_CUST_SALES_EXT_T -> MIG_CMDS_EI_CUST_SALES_EXT_S)

Run transaction SE11 and display structure CMDS_EI_SALES_DATA.

This structure is part APIs, WebServices, ….
(Navigation path from Maintain Business Partner class CL_MD_BP_MAINTAIN parameter: I_DATA -> CVIS_EI_EXTERN -> CUSTOMER -> SALES_DATA -> SALES -> DATA)

Add Z-field to FIORI app

Add new field to your FIORI app  "Manage Customer Master" (F0850A) (SAP Fiori Apps Reference Library (ondemand.com)) at Sales Area level.

Start FIORI app from FIORI Launchpad and navigate into customer sales area data.

Switch into "Adapt UI" mode via your profile.

To add a new Field Group use right mouse-click at your target area and "Create: Group" option appears.

Enter a text for your new group and use right mouse-click at your group name to add a new field.

At appearing pop-up you can find your new field. It might be that you are forced to scroll a bit. In my example my new field is available without scrolling.

Mark this field and press OK button. By using the mouse you can place the field group.
Don't forget to save and activate you changes. By activation system is asking you for a version name. Enter a name and confirm.

By pressing button "Publish Version" you can take over your changes into a transport request to publish your changes to your landscape.

Testing

Leave the "Adapt UI" mode and test your new field.

Using calendar button you can select a date out of the calendar pup-up to take over a specific date.

Apply and save you changes and navigate into Sales Area data in display mode to check your changes.

As a last technical check navigate into transaction SE16N and use table KNVV. Don't forget to remember customer number and used Sales Area.

Optional: Add field to BP transaction

Now you have done technical prerequisites to add the new field to BP transaction which is not done via field extensibility automatically.

If you want to implement this new field into BP transaction, follow instructions at blog post SAP S/4HANA Business Partner - Field Enhancement. You can directly step into topic "Implementation".

entry.title: SAP Sovereign Cloud Capabilities and What Sets Us Apart
entry.content:
Author – Subhranshu Sekhar

Introduction

In this post, we will explore SAP's sovereign cloud capabilities, what sets us apart, sovereign cloud solutions portfolio, deployment model and the critical aspects of security in SAP’s sovereign Cloud capabilities, as well as best practices to ensure your data remains protected.

This is a first of a series of blogs about SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities.

SAP's Definition of Sovereign Cloud

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are specialised cloud services designed to meet the unique security, compliance, and data sovereignty requirements of organisations operating in regulated environments. This includes a cloud computing architecture designed to enforce strict compliance with a country’s laws and regulations regarding data residency and data sovereignty. This cloud service ensures that data resides within specific geopolitical boundaries, providing enhanced control and protection against unauthorized access.

Key Differentiators

  • Cloud services align with government standards or policies to support requirements to certify or authorise the services for use
  • Security-cleared personnel manage cloud computing and operations infrastructure
  • Operations take place in government accredited security facilities. 
  • A local control plane and local automation ensure that organisations can handle data self-sufficiently within their country
  • Subsequent self-sufficiency ensures resilience and maintenance of control and security over cloud workloads, no matter the geopolitical circumstances.

Sovereign Cloud Portfolio

The SAP Intelligent Sustainable Enterprise Suite provides next-generation customer experience by delivering intelligent and innovative solutions for every line of business; these are end-to-end business processes that are modular, integrated, and span across different business solutions by infusing Generative AI use cases . In this blog we will be touching some of the key LoB’s within our current sovereign cloud capabilities

Security

SAP's approach to security in the sovereign cloud is fundamentally based around 3 pillars

  1. Build Securely
  2. Run Securely
  3. Act Securely

Some of the key security features in SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are:

  • Data sovereignty and localization, including stricter access controls
  • Regulatory compliance and regular audits built to comply with Australian ISM controls and all solutions in scope are already IRAP assessed and operated  out of a secure Zone 3 and Zone 4 facility
  • Enhanced security controls include encryption, multi-factor authentication, role based access control, advanced threat detection and response

Customer workloads across all LOB's are secured and protected using a shared responsibility model where SAP’s sovereign cloud operations are responsible for implementing preventative controls which range from helpful to restrictive. SAP’s sovereign cloud compliance and security is aligned to the NIST Framework

SAP’s Sovereign Cloud Capabilities in S/4 HANA Private Cloud Edition

 

 

As the cornerstone of RISE with SAP,  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition can safeguard your existing ERP investment while you benefit from the latest innovations and business-wide flexibility. The RISE with SAP offering includes an AI-enabled cloud ERP that's managed and optimized by SAP using a clean core approach. RISE with SAP also offers solutions for optimizing processes, extending and enhancing capabilities, and running sustainably.

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are managed services and deployments of security-hardened SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition solutions designed to adhere to national security and secrecy requirements such as government accreditation, attestation, or certification programs. They are designed for SAP customers who require additional assurances for their cloud workloads and cloud delivery model, including those in the public sector and regulated industries. With sovereign cloud capabilities in SAP S/4 HANA Cloud Private Edition, we provide innovative offerings in a more secure sovereign environment, enabling you to comply with the unique regulations specific to your country.

 

BTP

Built upon a Foundation of Trust

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities leverage SAP's Business Technology Platform architecture, a proven and secure solution already trusted by organizations worldwide. We take this robust foundation a step further by implementing additional security measures and controls specifically designed to meet the stringent requirements of Australian and New Zealand government departments and critical infrastructure providers

Unparalleled Security and Local Expertise

Your data is our top priority. Our dedicated, Australia-based team provides support for the platform, ensuring your information always remains secure.  Data is stored across geographically redundant data centres, adhering to the highest security standards.

Up to Date and Available

SAP’s sovereign cloud capabilities are built from the ground up for continuous availability. Most updates are deployed in real-time, minimizing disruption to your operations. We leverage industry-leading monitoring and tools to track performance across all cloud components. In the unlikely event of an issue, we are able to leverage our global support team to quickly react and solve the problem, restoring the system’s functionality.

Global Innovation, Locally Delivered

Our global development teams are constantly working to introduce new features and enhancements to the platform. These advancements undergo rigorous security assessments by our dedicated team before being deployed to your environment by local experts. This ensures you benefit from cutting-edge innovation whilst maintaining the highest security posture.

 

 

HCM

 SAP SuccessFactors HCM is a truly global suite of cloud HR solutions that gives you everything you need to fuel employee success and drive organizational agility at scale. Built on SAP’s trusted innovation platform and powered by a single source of people and skills data, it provides the foundation and flexibility required to improve every people experience, decision, and process across your entire business.

Elevate every experience. Increase employee engagement and productivity by delivering individualized experiences designed for how, when, and where people work.

Create a future ready workforce. Boost performance and retention by finding, developing, and mobilizing the skills, attributes, and leaders needed for today and tomorrow.

Enable agile and compliant HR. Improve organizational agility by supporting all people in all locations across all ways of working with the flexibility to quickly expand and adapt.

Increase the strategic value of HR. Achieve more faster with less risk, complexity, and cost by adopting a cloud-first approach backed by technology, partnership, and community.

SAP SuccessFactors offerings within sovereign cloud are designed for SAP customers who require additional assurances for their cloud workloads and cloud delivery model, including those in the public sector and regulated industries. Security-cleared nationals manage cloud computing and operations infrastructure out of certified security facilities.

Our sovereign cloud solutions are designed to meet key audit and regulatory standards.

 

As more offerings and roadmap items are released within SAP sovereign cloud capabilities , future blogs will be covering those updates

This blog is also a contribution from following team members:

Kirsten Tucker – Sovereign cloud ANZ head of Delivery

Hadi Zadeh – Sovereign cloud ANZ ISSO

Warrick Howieson - Sovereign cloud ANZ BTP Core Lead

Bill Xie - Sovereign cloud ANZ BTP Extensions Lead

Abhishek Gudup - Sovereign cloud ANZ HCM Lead

entry.title: MDG Cloud ready Mode: Integration of S/4HANA with SAP Build process automation & SAP Build work zone
entry.content:
Hi All,

In October 2023, SAP launched a new operational mode in MDG "CLOUD-READY MODE" for S/4 HANA 2023, following the principles of ABAP cloud and clean core methodology. Cloud-ready mode is only available for Business partners with Product domains in the MDG future roadmap. 

I briefly wrote about how this mode works in a previous blog post, hence if you have missed that you find the link below

Introducing MDG cloud ready mode for Business partners in S/4 HANA 2023

INTRODUCTION:

To setup cloud ready mode, we have following pre-requisites:

  1. An S/4 HANA 2023 private cloud edition system.
  2. A BTP tenant with at least one subaccount.
  3. Running instances of SAP build process automation, SAP Build workzone (standard Edition) and cloud connector

In this blog post, I will provide a detailed step by step approach to setup SAP build process automation and backend S/4 HANA 2023 for cloud ready mode.

MAIN CONTENT

BTP configuration:

  1.  We start by subscribing to SAP build process automation and SAP build workzone( standard edition ) in the BTP cockpit.
  2.  Assign the below role collections to your user. 

  3. On the destination tab in BTP cockpit, create the WM_CF_SPACE_PROVIDER destination. This destination will be used to import the live process packages in SAP build process automation.

    Note: The cloud foundry API end point and the URL for the destination should match as given in the SAP help portal. The user can be a generic user who doesn't have 2 factor authentication enabled. The details can be found Default Identity Provider | SAP Help Portal

     
  4. From Instances tab, launch the SAP build process automation lobby and import the Master data process approval & visualization project from the Store.

  5. Activate the 5 visibility scenarios, which we will use at a later stage.

     

  6. In the Instances tab in BTP cockpit, check sap_processautomation instance is created. Click on this and create a service key for the backend S4 HANA system.

     

  7. Assign MDG-specific BTP role collections to your user.

  8. The next step would be to setup the SAP build workzone. For the sap_processautomation instance create a service key for the workzone. 

  9. Once the key is created, create a destination for workzone. For details on how various parameters of the destination will be set, please refer to Configure SAP Build Process Automation Destinations | SAP Help Portal

     

  10. From the instances & subscription tag, launch the SAP build workzone standard edition application.
  11. In workzone, navigate to the content manager and choose the below 5 apps.
  12.  Create a local copy of the Visibility Scenario Dashboard app, change the title to Process Path Overview, and provide the navigation parameter scenarioID with the value com.sap.mdg.pv.ProcessVis.

     

  13. Create a group MDG and enable the My inbox and process path overview apps
  14. Assign the permissions for this 5 apps to everyone.
  15. Create a site with the same alias name as the group we created in step 13.

     

  16. Create the below destinations in BTP for your workflow and process path overview. Configure BTP Destinations | SAP Help Portal

     

S/4 HANA on-premise configuration:

Cloud ready mode should be activated in the S/4 HANA system, once activated classic MDG related apps for mass processing and consolidation will stop working and the new consolidation & mass processing apps for S/4 HANA 2023 needs to be used.

1.  Create an SM59 destination in S/4 HANA system and provide the host by pasting the same hostname as the MDG workzone site. The path prefix should be /site/mdg.

2. Create an entry in the SM30 view /UI2/V_ALIASMAP as below.

3. Go to MDGIMG-> cloud-ready mode and follow the path as per below screenshot to begin with integration of Build process automation with MDG. The subsequent steps will cover all the configurations in detail.

4. In Maintain the destination to an external server, create a destination MDG_BTP_SBPA_API. The host will be same as the API endpoint for the service key created in step6 of the BTP configuration section. You have to set SSL to active.

5. Now we maintain the OAuth Client 2.0 for secure communication between SBPA & S4 HANA.

Create a new profile configuration:
For the OAuth 2.0 client profile, choose SWF_CPPA_OAUTH_PROFILE. Enter a configuration name, for example, the name of the subaccount of your service.
For the client ID, enter the JSON value $.uaa.clientid from your service key card.
Enter the client secret by copying it from the BTP instance sap_processautomation service key for S4 HANA system.
Navigate to the Authorization Server Settings section, paste the uaa-URL from your service key to the Authorization Endpoint field, and add the /oauth/authorize suffix. In the same section, add the uaa-URL from your service key to the Token Endpoint field, and add the /oauth/token suffix.

6. Activate the following OData Services:
• MASTERDATACHANGEPROCESSSB
• C_MDCHGPROCESSBPMATCH_SRV
• SWF_CPWF_NOTIFICATION_SRV
• SWF_CPPV_INTEGRATION_SRV

7.  Authorization needs to be assigned to the user which will communicate between S4 HANA and SBPA. I have used a generic user with all the MDG roles. Hence not sharing the details for this step. 

8.  We maintain the connectivity with workflow capability. The configuration name is same as the configuration done for OAuth 2.0 client configuration in step 5

9.Maintain connectivity for generic event API of visibility capability. The configuration name is same as the configuration done for OAuth 2.0 client configuration in step 5.

10. Create a destination which will call back the S/4 HANA system. Here the URL should be the same as the one exposed in the cloud connector. The generic user should have sufficient authorizations. The user I used has all the MDG roles, hence not shared in detail.

CONCLUSION:

By following through the steps mentioned in this blog post, you will be able to setup MDG cloud ready mode with a fully functional workflow in SAP build process automation. 

Cloud ready mode is the stepping-stone for next-gen master data management providing features like Data federation, integration with address provider CDQ.

Hope this will help you start your MDG cloud ready mode journey.

Feel free to reach out if you have any questions.

SAP Master Data Governance SAP Build Process Automation  SAP Build Work Zone, standard edition SAP S/4HANA  SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition SAP Business Technology Platform ABAP Cloud SAP S/4HANA business partner 

Regards

Anirban

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Conversion – CVI_PRECHK - Tax Type XXX is not maintained for country YY
entry.content:
Introduction

The first step in converting your system from SAP ERP to SAP S/4HANA is the Readiness Check, which includes the Simplification Item list. One of the items on the list is "S4TWL - Business Partner Approach" in the LoB/Technology "Database and Data Management," which focuses on the conversion and activation of CVI (Customer Vendor Integration).

This includes "Data Correction" to analyze and correct customer/vendor master data.

As part of the Readiness Check, a data quality check is also conducted, and the results can be found at the specified location.

Navigating to

During this analysis, most customers will encounter errors that are caused by missing customizing settings and can be easily resolved.

With this blog I will try to give you a guideline how to resolve this errors.

 

Background

Since SAP S/4HANA does not allow transactions like XD03 to access customer data, the Business Partner transaction BP is used. Additionally, there have been changes in how tax numbers are stored, tax numbers now are stored in their own table and synchronized with corresponding fields in customer/vendor master data.

Business Partner Tax Number table: DFKKBPTAXNUM
Customer Tax Numbers: KNA1
Vendor Tax Numbers: LFA1

A key element of the Business Partner concept is the Tax Number Category, which follows a specific logic and is shared across application-specific data. To ensure proper mapping, the definition of Tax Number Categories for each customer/vendor country in the master data is essential.

To differentiate between different tax numbers Tax Number Category has been introduced at BP transaction which follows the logic of this example:
DE0
- 3 digits (exception is Argentina with some categories with 4 digits)
- First 2 digits: short country code for which tax number is valid -> e.g. DE
- 1 digit: type of tax number -> representing tax number type of customer/vendor

At this screenshot you will see the main mapping logic from customer to Business Partner. The mapping is similar of vendor to Business Partner.

To fulfill this mapping logic you need the definition of Tax Number Categories for each customer/vendor country of your master data.
To identify missing Tax Number Categories in your system, the CVI Master Data Consistency Check report within the CVI-Cockpit can be run. This report can also be called without the CVI-Cockpit by using transaction CVI_PRECHK.

 

Master Data Consistency Check report

The report will display any master data errors, and the best approach is to download the error log to Excel, remove duplicates, and then add the missing tax types through customization.

I restricted selection to one customer (AM006) only. You will see a similar error log:

 

Add missing Tax Number Categories

Tax Number Category definition has two levels of configuration.
The first level of Tax Type definition involves defining Tax Types for each country at the V_TFKTAXNUMTYPE maintenance view via transaction SM30.
The second level is to add Tax Number Categories used in the BP transaction.

 

Maintain V_TFKTAXNUMTYPE

At this maintenance view you define Tax Types for each country. Usually all Tax Number Categories are part of standard delivery content of client 000. As reference you can use SAP Note 775919 - Missing tax number categories of business partner or you can use content of table at the end of this post.

Please pay attention to this statement of the SAP Note
"The function module for those tax number types ending with '0' (eg. DE0, AT0) is VAT_REGISTRATION_NUMBER_CHECK. For the others, assign the function module BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK. For tax number types AR1A, AR1B, AR1C, AR1D, AR1E, AR1F and AR1Z assign '80', '86', '87', 96, 91, 94 and 99 correspondingly to the field STCDT in the view."

 

Maintain V_TFKTAXNUMTYPEC

After initial declaration of Tax Number Categories, the specific Categories that can be entered in the Business Partner master data must be defined at the V_TFKTAXNUMTYPEC maintenance view.

 

Rerun Master Data Consistency Check report

After making these adjustments, the Master Data Consistency Check report can be rerun out of Run History, and at this point, all Tax Number Category errors should be resolved.

 

Content of view V_TFKTAXNUMTYPE

TAXTYPECHECKFCTSTCDTADDRESS_DEPName
AD3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Andorra: Tax Register Number (NRT)
AE0VAT_REGISTRATION_NUMBER_CHECK  UAE: VAT Registration Number
AE1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  UAE: Tax Identification Number (TIN)
AF1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Afghanistan: Tax Identification Number
AG1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Antigua and Barbuda: VAT Reg. Number
AI3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Anguilla: Tax Identification Number
AL1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Albania: VAT Registration Number
AM1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Armenia: VAT Registration Number
AN1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Dutch Antilles: TIN Number
AO0BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Angola: Tax Identification Number (NIF)
AO3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Angola: Tax Identification Number (NIF)
AQ1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Antarctica: Tax Identification Number
AR1ABUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK80XArgentina: CUIT Number
AR1BBUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK86 Argentina: CUIL Number
AR1CBUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK87 Argentina: CDI Number
AR1DBUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK96 Argentina: DNI Number
AR1EBUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK91 Argentina: Foreign Identification card
AR1FBUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK94 Argentina: Passport Number
AR1ZBUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK99 Argentina: without ID
AR2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Argentina: Income Tax Number
AR3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  **bleep** number or CM number
AS3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Samoa, America: Tax Identification Numbe
AT0VAT_REGISTRATION_NUMBER_CHECK  Austria: VAT Registration Number
AU0VAT_REGISTRATION_NUMBER_CHECK  Australia: ABN
AU1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Australia: Tax File Number (TFN)
AU2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Australia:Australian Company Number(ACN)
AW3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Aruba: Tax Identification Number
AZ3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Azerbaijan: Tax Identification Number
BA1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bosnia-Herzegovina: VAT Reg. Number
BB3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Barbados: Tax Identification Number
BD1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bangladesh: VAT Registration Number
BE0VAT_REGISTRATION_NUMBER_CHECK  Belgium: VAT Registration Number
BE1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belgium: Enterprise Number
BE2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belgium: VAT Reg. No. w/o country code
BE3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belgium: Tax Identification Number (TIN)
BE4BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belgium: ERS Selfbilling Number
BF3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Burkina Faso: Tax Identification Number
BG0VAT_REGISTRATION_NUMBER_CHECK  Bulgaria: Vat Registration Number
BG1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bulgaria: Unified ID Code
BG2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bulgaria: Personal ID
BG3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bulgaria: Social Security Number
BH0VAT_REGISTRATION_NUMBER_CHECK  Bahrain: VAT Registration Number
BI3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Burundi: Tax Identification Number
BJ3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Benin: Tax Identification Number
BL1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  San Bartolome: TIN Number
BM3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bermuda: Tax Identification Number
BN3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Brunei Daruss.: TIN Number
BO1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bolivia: Tax Identification Number
BQ3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bonaire, Saba: Tax Identification Number
BR1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Brazil: CNPJ Number
BR2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Brazil: CPF Number
BR3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Brazil: State Tax Number
BR4BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Brazil: Municipal Tax Number
BS1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bahamas: VAT Registration Number
BT3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Bhutan: Tax Identification Number
BW1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Botswana: VAT Registration Number
BW5BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Botswana: New VAT Number
BY1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belarus: VAT Registration Number
BZ1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belize: Social Security Number
BZ2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belize: Tax identifier (free use)
BZ5BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Belize: Tax Identification Number
CA1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Canada: Goods and Service Tax (GST) No.
CA2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Canada: Business Number
CA3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Canada: Provincial Sales Tax (PST) No.
CA4BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Canada: Quebec Sales Tax (QST) No.
CC3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Coconut Islands: TIN Number
CD3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Dem. Rep. Congo: TIN Number
CF3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Central African Republic: TIN Number
CG3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Rep.of Congo: Tax Identification Number
CH1BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Switzerland: UID Number
CH2BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Switzerland: VAT Number
CI3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Cote d'Ivoire: Tax Identification Number
CK3BUPA_TAX_NUMBER_CHECK  Cook Islands: Tax Identification Number

entry.title: Learning and Enablement in SAP Projects
entry.content:
As businesses continue to navigate the complexities of digital transformation, effective project management and streamlined processes have become crucial. SAP Activate provides a comprehensive methodology to support this journey, and within this framework, the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer stands out as an invaluable tool.

The SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer provides a variety of resources, such as accelerators, templates, and guides, that are relevant to each phase of an SAP project. These resources are designed to provide teams with knowledge and tools they need to execute their projects effectively, from planning to deployment and beyond. Specific content focusing on learning and enablement activities within SAP implementation projects is available in the SAP Activate Roadmap Viewer.

Some of the key learning and enablement deliverables include:

  • Enablement Strategy: Focusing on understanding the current learning and enablement capabilities (such as: is there an existing training and enablement team, tools and processes) to support a successful rollout of new learning and enablement plans. Defining enablement activities that are needed to support the project.
  • Learning Needs Analysis for Project Team: Identifying the project team’s learning needs and proposing learning paths based on their roles and the project’s objectives, evaluating knowledge gaps and recommending future learning paths.
  • Learning Needs Analysis for Users: Identifying learning and enablement requirements, analyzing digital learning opportunities, and thoroughly evaluating formal and informal ‘Push and Pull’ learning methodologies ensure that the right learning is available when needed.
  • Enablement Content Development: Creating readily accessible, easily digestible, and end-user specific enablement content ensures the effective adoption of solution knowledge and skills needed by the organization’s end users, contributing to higher system adoption and productivity.
  • Enablement Delivery: Covering the various methods of enablement delivery available to an organization, including onsite and virtual classroom learning, e-learning self-enablement, webinars, social collaboration, blogs, and on-the-job coaching.

This is a game-changer for project teams navigating the complexities of learning and enablement within SAP implementations. By providing easy access to curated, customizable resources, it empowers teams to work more efficiently, collaborate effectively, and reduce the risks associated with complex projects. Whether you are starting your SAP journey, or are deep into the deployment phase, the Roadmap Viewer’s learning and enablement content can help you achieve your enablement project goals with confidence.
